_doc: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for FanOps multi-agent lane orchestration. Edit THIS file to add/retire a lane or re-assign a hot file for a wave — nothing else. Two independent consumers: (1) scripts/lane_guard.py — OFFLINE, at pre-push + CI: maps branch->lane by prefix, then FAILS a push/PR that edits a hot file owned by a DIFFERENT lane (this is the mechanical enforcement of the AGENTS.md 'disjoint hot files' rule). (2) the fanops-orchestrator agent — ONLINE: uses each lane's `linear` block to pull the next READY ticket from Linear at runtime instead of a frozen list. Discovered Linear facts (2026-07-07): team 'Molham homsi'; statuses = Backlog | Todo | In Progress | In Review | Done | Canceled | Duplicate; every issue carries a canonical `gitBranchName`; active wave = project 'FanOps: CI Hardening (2026 Audit)' (MOL-181..199).

guard._doc: The guard is OPT-IN by branch prefix: a branch whose name does not start with a lane prefix below is IGNORED (exit 0). So normal branches (cursor/*, bycreamco/mol-*, human branches) are never blocked. Only files listed in `hot_files` are restricted; every other path is unrestricted. `owner` may be a single lane or a list (a genuinely shared hot file, coordinated in TIME by the orchestrator, not by this static guard).

linear.note: Set `project` or `labels` per wave to the Linear grouping for publish-resilience work; leave empty to let the orchestrator hand this lane tickets explicitly.

picking.owns_note: Clip/moment/casting generation core (models, crosspost, ledger, casting, clip, moments, prompts).

linear.note: Set per wave (e.g. the picking-rebuild PRD label).

rfd.owns_note: Root-fix cross-cutting lane; shares moments.py/prompts.py with picking (TIME-coordinated by the orchestrator, never run concurrently on those files).

ci.owns_note: CI / infra / tooling. No src/fanops hot files, so it is compatible with any source lane running in parallel.
